title: Glorious Things of Thee are Spoken

metadata.composer: 'Austria (Haydn)' or 'Austrian Hymn' Franz Joseph Haydn, 1797 "The Evangelical Hymnal with Tunes", 1880
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2014 Revision.
c_lines: Music: 'Austria (Haydn)' or 'Austrian Hymn' Franz Joseph Haydn, 1797.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2014 Revision.

1.raw_text: Glorious things of thee are spoken, Zion city of our God; He, Whose Word cannot be broken Formed thee for His Own abode: On the Rock of Ages founded, What can shake thy sure repose? With salvation's walls surrounded, Thou mayst smile at all thy foes.

2.raw_text: See, the streams of living waters, Springing from Eternal Love, Well supply thy sons and daughters, And all fear of want remove: Who can faint, while such a river Ever will their thirst assuage? Grace, which, like the Lord the Giver, Never fails from age to age.

3.raw_text: Round each habi- tation hov'ring, See the cloud and fire appear, For a glory and a cov'ring: Showing that the Lord is near. Thus deriving from their banner Light by night, and shade by day, Safe they feed upon the manna, Which He gives them on their way.

4.raw_text: Savior, if of Zion's city I, through grace, a member am, Let the world deride or pity, I will glory in Thy Name: Fading is the worldling's pleasure, All his boasted pomp and show: Solid joys and lasting treasure None but Zion's children know.
